_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in integrating software Test & Evaluation frameworks into test strategies for specific projects.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing automated tests for data applications, including unit tests for transformation logic, integration tests for pipeline components, and data validation tests using appropriate testing frameworks.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-002 (RM, cosine: 0.504), AB-S-003 (AB, cosine: 0.484).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.1/10 (raw 0.708). Shared vocabulary: [frameworks, skill]. DCWF-only terms [evaluation, integrating, projects, software, specific, strategies] vs WIDAI-only terms [applications, appropriate, automated, components, data, integration] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.893 (P=0.901, R=0.885). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.9%). "Skill in integrating software Test & Evaluation frameworks into test strategies" and "Skill in writing automated tests for data applications, including unit tests for" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.631 < STS 0.708 (gap -0.077). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [evaluation, integrating, projects] and [applications, appropriate, automated] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.8%. Shared tokens: [frameworks, skill]. DCWF-only: [evaluation, integrating, projects, software, specific, strategies]. WIDAI-only: [applications, appropriate, automated, components, data, integration].
